Chlorine does not rise despite dosing: Chlorine does not rise despite dosing
Algae visible on walls or floor: Algae visible on walls or floor
Skin or eye irritation when swimming: Skin or eye irritation when swimming
Always proceed step by step: measure fir: Always proceed step by step: measure first → correct in small steps → let the water circulate → measure again.
Always follow the product instructions (: Always follow the product instructions (concentration and pool volume may differ).
If you are unsure, correct more slowly r: If you are unsure, correct more slowly rather than forcing it.
Problem: pH value is too low (water is t: Problem: pH value is too low (water is too acidic).
A pH value that is too low can attack ma: A pH value that is too low can attack materials (metals / built-in parts) more strongly and can increase irritation during bathing. It can also make the overall water balance unstable.
A lot of rainwater / fresh water (can ac: A lot of rainwater / fresh water (can acidify the water)
Low alkalinity (buffer missing → pH drop: Low alkalinity (buffer missing → pH drops faster)
Raise the pH gradually with a pH increas: Raise the pH gradually with a pH increaser / pH plus product.
If the pH keeps dropping again, also che: If the pH keeps dropping again, also check total alkalinity / the buffer, otherwise every pH correction will only hold for a short time.
Problem: pH value is too high (water is: Problem: pH value is too high (water is too alkaline).
If the pH value is too high, chlorine /: If the pH value is too high, chlorine / disinfection often works less effectively and cloudiness / precipitation (for example lime) can occur more easily. Eyes and skin may also become irritated.
Strong outgassing / a lot of bubbling (C: Strong outgassing / a lot of bubbling (CO₂ escapes → pH rises)
Some pools / materials can initially pus: Some pools / materials can initially push the pH upward
Lower the pH in small steps with a pH re: Lower the pH in small steps with a pH reducer / pH minus product.
If the pH repeatedly rises again, also c: If the pH repeatedly rises again, also consider alkalinity / water hardness, because this strongly affects stability.
Problem: Chlorine level is too low.
Too little effective chlorine can allow: Too little effective chlorine can allow germs and algae to multiply more easily. Often the first signs are tired-looking water: less clarity, more odor, and deposits forming faster.
A lot of sun / UV exposure (chlorine bre: A lot of sun / UV exposure (chlorine breaks down faster)
High bathing load (sweat / organic matte: High bathing load (sweat / organic matter consumes chlorine)
Unfavorable pH value (chlorine works les: Unfavorable pH value (chlorine works less effectively)
Bring disinfection back into a normal ra: Bring disinfection back into a normal range and check the pH in parallel.
If chlorine keeps dropping permanently,: If chlorine keeps dropping permanently, also check causes such as organic load, filter condition, and circulation.
Problem: Chlorine level is too high.
A chlorine level that is too high can ir: A chlorine level that is too high can irritate eyes and skin, cause strong odor, and put more stress on materials and covers. In this situation, adding even more chemicals is almost never the right direction.
Too much chlorine added / shock treatmen: Too much chlorine added / shock treatment too high
Too little natural breakdown (cover on +: Too little natural breakdown (cover on + little sun)
Let time do the work (sun / UV reduces c: Let time do the work (sun / UV reduces chlorine) and keep the water circulating well.
Only evaluate further corrections (for e: Only evaluate further corrections (for example pH) once chlorine is back in a normal range.
Problem: Chlorine level does not rise de: Problem: Chlorine level does not rise despite dosing.
This often happens when the water has a: This often happens when the water has a high chlorine demand: the added chlorine is consumed immediately before a stable free chlorine level can remain measurable.
High organic load (dirt, biofilm, heavy: High organic load (dirt, biofilm, heavy use)
First check the basic values (pH, filter: First check the basic values (pH, filter / circulation, visual inspection for deposits).
Get the water clean again (clean / backw: Get the water clean again (clean / backwash the filter, brush the pool), so chlorine is not only being consumed immediately.
If no effect can be measured permanently: If no effect can be measured permanently, there is often an oxidative demand present (chlorine is immediately bound / broken down) – then only consistent reduction of the causes and measuring again will help.
Problem: Strong chlorine smell despite t: Problem: Strong chlorine smell despite the measured value.
The typical indoor pool smell often does: The typical indoor pool smell often does not come from too much good chlorine, but from bound chlorine compounds (chloramines). These are formed when chlorine combines with nitrogen / organic matter from sweat and similar contamination.
Too little fresh water / breakdown / fil: Too little fresh water / breakdown / filter care
Poor ventilation (for indoor pools) incr: Poor ventilation (for indoor pools) increases the perceived smell
The goal is to reduce bound contaminatio: The goal is to reduce bound contamination: good circulation / filtration, pool cleaning, and possibly a fresh water share.
Also check the pH in parallel, because a: Also check the pH in parallel, because an incorrect pH further reduces disinfection performance.
Green water is very often a sign of alga: Green water is very often a sign of algae growth (often caused by too little effective chlorine and / or poor circulation).
Free chlorine too low or already consume: Free chlorine too low or already consumed
pH too high → chlorine works much less e: pH too high → chlorine works much less effectively
Insufficient filter / circulation or dir: Insufficient filter / circulation or dirty filter
High temperature + sun (algae growth is: High temperature + sun (algae growth is encouraged)
Check the water values (especially pH an: Check the water values (especially pH and chlorine) and improve circulation / filter condition.
Brush / vacuum the pool thoroughly so de: Brush / vacuum the pool thoroughly so deposits do not survive.
Then let the filter run patiently until: Then let the filter run patiently until the water becomes clear again.
Problem: Water is cloudy / gray / milky.
Cloudy water is often caused by fine sus: Cloudy water is often caused by fine suspended particles or chemical imbalances (for example pH / alkalinity / lime) that the filter cannot handle well.
Dirty filter / filter runtime too short: Dirty filter / filter runtime too short
pH / alkalinity outside the stable range: pH / alkalinity outside the stable range
High water hardness (lime) – especially: High water hardness (lime) – especially together with high pH
Too little disinfection → biological clo: Too little disinfection → biological cloudiness is possible
Check the filter condition (cleaning / b: Check the filter condition (cleaning / backwashing) and ensure sufficient circulation.
Check the basic values pH / chlorine and: Check the basic values pH / chlorine and stabilize them.
If the cloudiness looks mineral (milky /: If the cloudiness looks mineral (milky / white), also consider water hardness / balance.
Problem: Algae visible on walls or floor: Problem: Algae visible on walls or floor.
Visible algae are a clear sign that disi: Visible algae are a clear sign that disinfection / circulation was not sufficient or that local dead zones are forming (corners / pipes).
Chlorine too low or already consumed: Chlorine too low or already consumed
pH too high (chlorine works less effecti: pH too high (chlorine works less effectively)
Too little brushing / pool care, poor ci: Too little brushing / pool care, poor circulation
Mechanics first: brush / vacuum consiste: Mechanics first: brush / vacuum consistently, maintain the filter.
Then stabilize the values (pH / chlorine: Then stabilize the values (pH / chlorine) and improve circulation.
The goal is to remove algae not only che: The goal is to remove algae not only chemically, but also mechanically.
Problem: Foam on the water surface.
Foam is often caused by surfactants / or: Foam is often caused by surfactants / organic matter (sunscreen, body care products, cleaning residues), some algaecides / polymers, or also air in the system. It is usually not a chlorine problem, but rather a substances-in-the-water problem.
A lot of lotions / oils in the water, hi: A lot of lotions / oils in the water, high organic load
Certain anti-algae products can promote: Certain anti-algae products can promote foam
Air entering the system (small leaks) →: Air entering the system (small leaks) → foam appears stronger
Skim off the surface, keep the filter cl: Skim off the surface, keep the filter clean, and reduce organic input.
Keep an eye on pH / chlorine as well (st: Keep an eye on pH / chlorine as well (stable values help break down organic matter faster).
If foam remains permanently, systematica: If foam remains permanently, systematically narrow down the source (products / contamination / technology).
Problem: Skin or eye irritation while sw: Problem: Skin or eye irritation while swimming.
Irritation can have several causes: pH t: Irritation can have several causes: pH that is too high or very low, too high a disinfectant concentration, or bound chlorine compounds (chloramines) caused by organic contamination.
Chlorine smell / chloramines caused by s: Chlorine smell / chloramines caused by strong organic input
Measure first (pH and chlorine) and brin: Measure first (pH and chlorine) and bring the values back into a normal range.
Keep the pool / filter clean so that org: Keep the pool / filter clean so that organic contamination does not turn into bound compounds.
If irritation is strong, pause swimming: If irritation is strong, pause swimming until the water values are stable.
Problem: Water values are often unstable: Problem: Water values are often unstable (change quickly).
If pH / chlorine fluctuate strongly all: If pH / chlorine fluctuate strongly all the time, the water often lacks stability: the buffer (alkalinity) is too low / too high, the load changes frequently, or circulation / filtration is not constant enough.
Total alkalinity too low → pH jumps / ti: Total alkalinity too low → pH jumps / tips over quickly
Many small corrections without enough ci: Many small corrections without enough circulation time
Poor filter condition / filter times too: Poor filter condition / filter times too short
Strong external influences (rain, heat,: Strong external influences (rain, heat, heavy use)
First get the basics stable (buffer / fi: First get the basics stable (buffer / filter / regularity), then only fine-tune.
Always correct in small steps and allow: Always correct in small steps and allow enough time for mixing.
Problem: It is unclear what exactly is w: Problem: It is unclear what exactly is wrong.
Visual check: Green? Cloudy? Deposits? F: Visual check: Green? Cloudy? Deposits? Foam?
Two measured values are enough for the s: Two measured values are enough for the start: pH + free chlorine.
Check the technology: Is circulation run: Check the technology: Is circulation running? Is the filter clean? Is backwashing needed?
Visual problems (green / cloudy) are ver: Visual problems (green / cloudy) are very often a combination of disinfection + pH + filtration.
Odor / irritation is often not too much: Odor / irritation is often not too much chlorine, but incorrectly bound / organically loaded water.
Note: This problem is not known yet.
Please select one of the existing items: Please select one of the existing items or use "Issue unclear / not specific".

solar_insights_summary_note_block_7: Block 7 writes direct reference values, estimated thermal power, estimated daily gain, estimated efficiency ratio, return sensor usage and weather-based evaluation flags.
solar_insights_calculation_note_block_7: Solar insights block 7 active: direct reference values, estimated thermal power, estimated daily gain, estimated efficiency ratio and weather-based evaluation flags are written.
solar_insights_debug_block_7_updated: Solar insights block 7 updated. Used sensors:
solar_insights_html_note_block_7: Estimated daily gain, estimated efficiency ratio, return sensor usage and weather-based evaluation flags are active.
solar_log_confidence_text: The current confidence of this assessment is about %s percent.
solar_log_text_no_runtime_today: Solar did not run today, so no meaningful solar gain can be reported for the day.
solar_log_text_used_sensors: The current assessment is based on these sensors and data sources: %s.
solar_log_text_active_effective: Solar is currently working effectively and is delivering about %s kW of thermal power.
solar_log_text_active_without_clear_gain: Solar is currently active, but no clearly usable heat gain can be identified at the moment.
solar_log_text_not_active_now: Solar is currently not actively contributing to pool heating.
solar_log_text_good_day: Today, solar has already made a strong contribution with an estimated heat gain of about %s kWh.
solar_log_text_moderate_day: Today, solar has already contributed a noticeable heat gain of about %s kWh.
solar_log_text_low_day: Today, solar has only provided a small estimated heat gain of about %s kWh so far.
solar_log_text_no_clear_day_gain: So far, no clearly usable daily solar gain can be derived from the available data.
solar_log_text_temperature_positive: The collector is currently at about %s °C, the pool reference is about %s °C, and the temperature advantage is about %s K.
solar_log_text_temperature_neutral: The collector temperature of about %s °C is currently very close to the pool reference temperature of about %s °C.
solar_log_text_temperature_negative: The collector temperature of about %s °C is currently below the pool reference temperature of about %s °C.
solar_log_text_flow_and_pump: The current estimated flow is about %s l/h with a pump power of about %s W.
solar_log_text_flow_only: The current estimated flow is about %s l/h.
solar_log_text_power_and_peak: The current estimated thermal power is about %s W, with a peak of about %s W reached today.
solar_log_text_efficiency_high: The current estimated efficiency ratio is about %s, which indicates a very good solar effect.
solar_log_text_efficiency_medium: The current estimated efficiency ratio is about %s, which indicates a usable solar effect.
solar_log_text_efficiency_low: The current estimated efficiency ratio is about %s, which indicates only a limited solar effect.
solar_log_text_active_minutes: Solar has been effectively active for about %s minutes today.
solar_log_text_outside_temp: The outside temperature currently used for context is about %s °C.
solar_log_text_weather_supported_with_te: Weather-based plausibility is active. Current weather summary: %s
solar_log_text_weather_supported: Weather-based plausibility is active for this assessment.
solar_log_text_small_gain_detail: The currently estimated daily gain is about %s Wh, which corresponds to about %s kWh.
solar_log_text_quality_level: The current quality level of the assessment is %s.
